newsSince: No material new company-specific news since June 9. The load-bearing catalysts - the Hexagon Robotics strategic partnership (rotary/strain-wave actuator supply plus a plan to deploy 1,000 AEON humanoids across Schaeffler factories by ~2032) and the Q1 2026 print (stable ~5% EBIT margin, ~30 prototype orders / 5 contracts, first series production in Q2 2026) - all predate June 9 and were already in the prior case. Stock drifted -4.5% since June 9.

sources.label: Schaeffler to deploy 1,000 Hexagon humanoids by 2032 (Robot Report)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the humanoid-stack page Schaeffler is a high-conviction actuation pick: direct Hexagon/AEON actuator supply + in-house factory deployment is among the page's strongest commercialization evidence. But the catalysts predate June 9 and were captured in the prior up-move; no fresh datapoint to extend, so hold the upgraded rank with a small positive tilt.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Hexagon AEON actuator supply + 1,000-robot factory deployment ramp through 2026-32.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Humanoid volumes/series-production timing slip; auto/industrial core cyclicality.
verdicts.bull: Direct, purest actuation play in the humanoid stack with both supply contract and captive factory deployment; ~50% of robot BOM is actuators.
verdicts.neutral: Strong stack fit but catalysts already known and priced; thin incremental news since June 9.
verdicts.bear: Humanoid revenue still tiny vs auto/industrial base; deployment timelines could slip and stock is a beta proxy.
